title: Interior Designer
description: What to ExpectThe purpose of the role of an Interior Designer is to create the design of future vehicle interiors, blending cutting-edge aesthetic concepts with functional engineering. This creative individual will play a key role in conceptualising and creating innovative and visually compelling interior designs, of both Concept and Production vehicles. They will collaborate closely with multiple Design and Engineering teams to ensure that design ideas are not only artistic, but are also feasible and aligned with brand identity. By staying ahead of design trends and exploring advanced new technologies, the Interior Designer will have an influential role in shaping the future of Mclaren interiors.What You'll DoGenerate Advanced Concepts and Production interior designs through sketches, digital renderings, and 3D modelsInterpret and evolve the brand’s interior design language into exciting, innovative, and usable interior spaces.Support design through all project phases — from early ideation through productionCollaborate with clay modelers, CAS teams, and engineering departments to refine proportions, and define design themes.Ensure aesthetic intent is maintained throughout the design process, integrating technical inputs while retaining design theme values and character.Guide surfaces with modelling teams for production alignment.Participate in design reviews, and digital/physical model evaluations.Act as a subject matter professional, supporting our expertise in interior designConstant collaboration with other Designers, Design management and other Design/ departments, for optimised and efficient design progress.Close collaboration with the Studio Engineering team to ensure that the interior design is not only aesthetically striking but also functional and manufacturable.What You'll BringYou have knowledge and hands-on experience in automotive interior design.You can evaluate, refine, and optimise automotive interiors, taking into account aesthetics and functionality.You can logically and efficiently break down challenges and propose innovative design solutions. You have a passion for pushing the boundaries of what’s possible.You intuitively demonstrate a drive to collaborate, and ensure consistent communication. Truly support a team culture and demonstrate positive enthusiasm.You work closely with the whole Design team to align on objectives and timing.You understand the differing requirements of concept design and production design, to ensure that interior design themes can be created to the highest standards.You are detail-oriented and committed to quality.You actively participate in demonstrating and defining the interior design values, exploring 2D and 3D designs, and guiding physical and digital/VR models.You have a passion for continuous improvement and learning, keeping up-to-date with emerging trends and innovations in technology, design, and the automotive market.You are always looking for ways to improve efficiency and workflow, showing a willingness to change and adapt.You are able to articulate complex design ideas and solutions.You maintain a collaborative and solution-focused attitude.Ability to generate original, forward-thinking design concepts that align with brand identity and future mobility trends.Advanced Sketching & Visualization: Exceptional freehand sketching and digital rendering skills to explore and communicate designs clearly.Advanced 3D visualization skills in software such as VRED or Blender, plus exceptional Photoshop abilities to produce powerful presentation visuals.3D Modelling Proficiency: Expertise in software such as Alias or Blender, for autonomously and rapidly creating and exploring concept themes. Blender or Alias SubD preferred.Consistent open communication with Interior Design team. Clear, considered design communications given to CAS and clay modelling teams to support efficient design development.Technical & Engineering Awareness: Ability to design within engineering and ergonomic constraints.Collaboration & Communication: Strong interpersonal skills to work effectively across design and engineering.Attention to Detail: High level of precision, refinement, and care in all stages of the design process.Time & Project Management: Ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment.Ability to anticipate future trends and translate them into bold, forward-looking design proposals and themes.Keen eye for form, proportion, and detail with an innate sense of visual harmony and brand expression.Ability to thrive under pressure and adapt to shifting priorities.Clear communicator who can present design ideas with clarity.Collaborative Spirit: Open-minded and inclusive, fostering a culture of teamwork and cooperation.Deep enthusiasm for automotive design, innovation, and shaping the future of mobility.

title: Lead Interior Designer
description: What to ExpectThe purpose of the role of a Lead Interior Designer is to lead and innovate the design of future vehicle interiors, blending cutting-edge aesthetic concepts with functional engineering. This creative individual will be responsible for conceptualising and creating visually compelling, ergonomically efficient, and market-relevant interior designs of both concept and production vehicles. They will collaborate closely with multiple Design and Engineering teams to ensure that design ideas are not only artistic but are also feasible and aligned with brand identity. By staying ahead of design trends and utilising advanced technologies, the Lead Interior Designer will play a key role in shaping the future of Mclaren interiors.What You'll Do- Generate advanced concept and production-ready interior designs through sketches, digital renderings, and 3D models- Support the refinement and development of selected design directions, retaining design values and character. Interpret and evolve the brand’s interior design language into exciting, functional, and usable interior spaces.- Drive design quality through all project phases — from early ideation to final surface release.- Collaborate closely with clay modelers, CAS teams, and engineering departments to refine proportions- Ensure aesthetic intent is maintained throughout the design process.- Guide surfaces with modelling teams for production alignment.- Participate in design reviews, supplier meetings, and digital/physical model evaluations.- Act as a subject matter expert on interior design, brand identity, surfacing techniques, and industry benchmarks.- Present design concepts and strategies internally to Design and Product ManagementWhat You'll Bring- You have knowledge and hands-on experience in automotive interior design, engineering, and materials.- You can evaluate, refine, and optimise automotive interiors, taking into account aesthetics, craftsmanship and functionality.- You stay ahead of trends inside and outside of automotive design, materials innovation and technologies.- You can logically and efficiently break down challenges and propose innovative design solutions.- You have a passion for pushing the boundaries of what’s possible.- You intuitively demonstrate a drive to collaborate, and ensure consistent communication.- You work closely with the whole Design team to align on objectives and timing.- You understand the differing requirements of concept design and production design, to ensure that interior design themes can be created to the highest standards.- You are detail-oriented and committed to quality, ensuring that the interior design and development processes meet both internal and external standards.- You actively participate in demonstrating and defining the interior design standards, guiding physical and digital/VR models.- As a Lead role, you provide guidance and technical expertise to other team members and departments. Your technical expertise is often relied upon.- You have a passion for continuous improvement and learning, keeping up-to-date with emerging trends and innovations in technology, design, and the automotive market.- You are always looking for ways to improve efficiency and workflow, showing a willingness to change and adapt- You are able to articulate complex design ideas, technical challenges, and solutions.

title: Interior Design Manager
description: What to ExpectThe purpose of the role of an Interior Design Manager is to lead the design and development of innovative and high-quality future vehicle interiors, blending cutting-edge aesthetic concepts with functional engineering. This individual will be responsible for conceptualising and creating visually compelling, ergonomically efficient, and market-relevant interior designs of both concept and production vehicles. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams, manage a talented group of designers, and ensure that our interiors not only meet customer expectations but also align with brand identity and market trends. You will have the opportunity to unleash your creativity and passion for automotive design while honing your leadership skills in a collaborative and supportive environment. This is an exciting opportunity to make a significant impact in the automotive industry. Build key relationships and collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop innovative Interior design concepts. You will work closely with other departments such as engineering, marketing, and manufacturing, ensuring that the interior design aligns with technical feasibility, market positioning, and production requirements. Presenting design proposals to internal stakeholders, executives, and external partners, the design manager effectively communicates the rationale behind design decisions and persuasively advocates for their implementation.What You'll DoThe Interior Design Manager supports senior design leadership in project planning, budgeting, and timeline management to ensure timely delivery of high-quality design solutions. Marketing and branding team collaboration is vital in aligning designs with consumer trends, brand identity, and market position. Frequent collaboration with ergonomics, PQ, HMI and UX, to ensure the designs are highly usable and built to the highest standards. Close collaboration with the engineering teams is crucial to ensure that the interior design is not only aesthetically striking but also functional and manufacturable. Present design concepts and strategies internally to design leadership and product management, effectively communicating the rationale behind decisions.Lead design reviews, supplier meetings, and digital/physical model evaluations. Reviews and guides surfaces with modelling teams for production alignment. Ensure aesthetic intent is maintained through feasibility reviews, tooling constraints, and engineering integration. Collaborate closely with clay modelers, CAS teams, and engineering departments to refine proportions and surface details. Drive design quality through all project phases — from early ideation to final surface release. Interpret and evolve the brand’s interior design language into exciting, functional, and usable interior spaces. Support the refinement and development of selected design directions, retaining design and engineering excellence. Lead a team of talent Interior designers to generate advanced and production-ready interior design concepts through sketches, digital renderings, and 3D models.What You'll BringYou have extensive knowledge and hands-on experience in automotive interior design, engineering, and materials.You can evaluate, refine, and optimise automotive interiors, taking into account aesthetics, craftsmanship and functionality.You stay ahead of trends inside and outside of automotive design, materials innovation and technologies.You can logically and efficiently break down complex challenges and propose innovative solutions, whether related to design, ergonomics, manufacturing processes, or engineering components.You enjoy working on technical details and have a passion for pushing the boundaries of what’s possible.You intuitively demonstrate a drive to collaborate, and ensure consistent communication across departments and teams.You manage a team of Interior Designers to align on objectives, timing and project requirements.You can bridge the gap between concept design and manufacturing, ensuring that the interior design vision can be achieved efficiently and to the highest standards.You are detail-oriented and committed to quality, ensuring that the interior design and development processes meet both internal and external standards.You actively participate in demonstrating and defining the interior design standards, validating and guiding physical and digital/VR models.You have a passion for continuous improvement and learning, keeping up-to-date with emerging trends and innovations in technology, design, and the automotive market.You are always looking for ways to improve efficiency and workflows, showing a willingness to change and adapt.You are able to articulate complex design ideas, technical challenges, and solutions to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including senior management and external partners.You provide clear and actionable recommendations while maintaining a collaborative and solution-focused attitude.You are able to build narrative and presentation stories to best sell design themes.Advanced Sketching & Visualization: Communicative freehand sketching and digital rendering to explore and communicate designs clearly, and 3D visualization skills alongside Photoshop to produce high-level presentation content visuals.Creative Concept Development: Ability to generate original, forward-thinking design concepts that align with brand identity and future mobility trends.3D Modelling Proficiency: Expertise in software such as Alias or Blender, for creating and exploring concept themes, as well as aiding communication with the CAS modelling team.Creative team communication: Clear, considered design direction given to Design team colleagues, CAS and clay modelling teams, to support efficient design development.Design Strategy: Skill in developing a compelling design narrative and translating it into impactful design concepts.Story Telling: Skilled in creation of PPT presentation content and narrative, to best sell ideas and design themes to a wide audience.Knowledge of Automotive Trends: Deep understanding of industry trends, competitor landscape, and consumer expectations.Technical & Engineering Awareness: Ability to design within manufacturing, safety, ergonomic, and regulatory constraints.Collaboration & Communication: Strong interpersonal skills to work effectively with engineering, marketing, and leadership teams.Attention to Detail: High level of precision, care and refinement in all stages of the design process.

title: Interior Creative Specialist - Automotive
description: At the cutting edge of innovation, Mahindra Advanced Design Europe (M.A.D.E) is where bold ideas meet world-class execution. Based in our purpose-built studio in Banbury, M.A.D.E plays a pivotal role in the Mahindra Global Design Network, shaping the future of electric vehicles and advanced automotive concepts. Our studio is equipped with the latest design technologies, enabling full-spectrum design capabilities - from initial concept sketches to 3D digital and physical modelling, A-Class surfacing and immersive visualisation. We also house a complete CMF suite, clay modelling studio, and dedicated presentation spaces. The Role: Interior Creative SpecialistAs an automotive interior designer or creative specialist, you'll use your imaginative and detail-driven creativity to shape the future of our in-car experiences. You'll develop innovative interior concepts that blend aesthetics, functionality, and brand identity.  You'll use your skills to ensure that we stay true to our design philosophy and vision, whilst contributing to the evolving legacy of Mahindra.What You'll Do:Develop compelling interior design concepts from early ideation through to production-ready proposalsCreate sketches, visualisations and ideation work with precision and intentCollaborate closely with cross-functional teams including Studio Engineering, CMF, and the Exteriors team to solve design challengesResearch emerging trends in automotive design and technology, with a particular focus on user experiencePresent concepts to stakeholders with clarity and confidence, using strong storytelling as your foundationSupport the refinement of interior components, focusing on feasibility without compromising creativityWhat You Bring:A degree in Transportation Design, Industrial Design or related fieldProven experience in automotive interior design or similar creative disciplineStrong sketching, visualisation, and storytelling skillsProficiency in design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite, Blender, VRED or similarA passion for innovation, craftsmanship and user-centric designAbility to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environmentWhat You'll Get We believe that great work deserves great rewards. As part of the Mahindra Group, you'll enjoy a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package, designed to support your well-being and growth. 26 days annual leave plus statutory holidays - because rest fuels creativityPrivate medical insurance - comprehensive coverage for peace of mindLife Assurance & Income Protection - security when it matters mostEnhanced Maternity & Sickness Benefits - supportive policies that reflect our commitment to your health and family lifeSalary Sacrifice Car Scheme - access to our EV schemeOn-Site Gym Access - stay active and energisedEmployee Assistance Programme - confidential support for life's challengesReward & Recognition Scheme - celebrating exceptional work and contributionsPerformance Bonus Scheme - your impact matters, and we make sure it's recognised Ready to Join Us? If you're passionate about pushing boundaries and being part of a team that values creativity, we'd love to hear from you.  Please share a copy of your portfolio showcasing your interior design work with your application.  Alternatively, please share a link or email a copy to the email address below.  Bring your expertise, your curiosity, and your collaborative spirit. We'll bring the trust, the challenges that stretch your thinking, and the opportunity to make your mark. Let's build something extraordinary - together.  For further details or questions, please contact maderecruit@mahindra.com

title: Event Manager
description: We are working with a highly regarded destination events and wedding venue to find an experienced and energetic Event Manager to join their senior operations team.Set within a breathtaking countryside estate, this venue is known for delivering standout weddings, celebrations, and corporate events, with a reputation built on exceptional service, attention to detail, and unforgettable guest experiences. This is an exciting opportunity for an events professional who thrives in a fast-paced, people-focused environment and enjoys seeing an event through from planning to flawless delivery.Please note: This is a destination venue so the ability to drive / having a car is a must for this role.The RoleAs Event Manager, you will take ownership of live event delivery across weddings, private celebrations, and corporate functions, ensuring every occasion runs seamlessly and exceeds expectations.Key responsibilities include:Leading the on-site coordination and execution of weddings, celebrations, and corporate eventsManaging all event logistics including room setup, catering flow, entertainment, and technical requirementsWorking closely with sales and planning teams to fully title: Event Manager
location: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description: About The Great Run Company“We started the Great North Run in 1981 because we were runners – and if you’re a runner you want everybody to run.” – Brendan Foster, President & Founder. The Great Run Company is the UK’s biggest mass participation sport business, home to the Great North Run, the world’s largest half marathon. Every year we inspire over 200,000 people to take part in events in cities all over the country accompanied by world class athletes. We are also home to FilmNova, the TV production arm of the Great Run Company, producing programmes and events for clients including BBC Sport, Channel 4, UEFA, Olympic Broadcasting Services, IAAF, European Athletics and British Triathlon.The Great Run Company welcomes everyone and anyone who is inspired to get active.Our valuesTeamwork, Excellence, Accountability and Ambition are lived and breathed by the team at Great run. We work hard as one team to ensure every event that we deliver is the best it can be, creating an amazing experience for our participants, partners, charities, and our employees.The role We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Events Manager to lead the delivery of medium to large-scale mass participation events within our portfolio. Due to the nature of the role you can be based remotely in the UK or from our Newcastle Head office (with hybrid working). This role does require regular UK wide travel.You will take full operational ownership of assigned events, ensuring they are delivered safely, on time, within budget, and to the highest possible standard. You will also provide primary operational support across other events within the portfolio as required.This is a fantastic opportunity to play a key role in delivering world-class live sporting events, including the internationally renowned Great North Run.Key ResponsibilitiesLead the planning, coordination, delivery and oversight of assigned event projectsTake operational ownership of key events to ensure delivery aligns with agreed objectivesWork closely with the Commercial team to support event activation and sponsorship feasibilityCollaborate with the Charity Coordinator to plan and deliver charity village operationsManage event budgets in partnership with Finance and Project Managers, proactively monitoring and controlling costs throughout the project lifecycleOversee delivery against the operational requirements of key stakeholders including sponsors, broadcasters, participants and audiencesLead engagement with local authorities and stakeholders, including Safety Advisory Groups, councils, police, highways, medical providers and building controlCoordinate with the Event Coordination team to recruit and deploy crew, volunteers, officials and timekeepersPlan for participant safety and welfare, including crowd flow management and detailed risk assessmentsDefine event task structures and support project planning, including resource allocationApply and champion company safe systems of workUndertake senior operational roles on event days to support the wider Operations teamEnsure compliance with relevant legislation and maintain up-to-date internal policies and proceduresAbout YouYou are an experienced live events professional with strong leadership capability and a deep understanding of UK road running or mass participation events. You are calm under pressure, highly organised and confident managing multiple stakeholders in complex environments.Essential Skills & ExperienceMinimum 3 years’ experience delivering live eventsStrong knowledge of UK road running events and associated safety requirementsExperience leading Safety Advisory Group (SAG) and external stakeholder meetingsExperience operating within a formal project management frameworkProven event team management experienceExperience managing event budgetsStrong stakeholder management skillsExcellent written and verbal communication skillsHighly organised with exceptional attention to detailAble to work to strict deadlines and remain calm under pressureProactive, adaptable and solutions-focusedWilling to work weekends and travel extensivelyFull UK driving licenseRight to work in the UKDesirableExperience delivering highway-based mass participation events (running, cycling, triathlon)Experience working on live televised eventsHow do I apply:If you are interested in the position, either apply via LinkedIn or send an email with your CV and a covering letter explaining what you will bring to the role to: hr@thegreatruncompany.com  At Great Run, we pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment. We are an equal opportunity employer, committed to embracing diversity and promoting equal opportunities for all, regardless of background, disability, religion, gender identity, sexuality, or ethnicity.  We welcome applications from individuals with disabilities and are dedicated to providing a supportive and accessible recruitment process. If you have a disability and meet the job criteria but require reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please do not hesitate to get in touch with us. We are here to ensure that every candidate has an equal opportunity to showcase their skills and potential.

title: People Lead – Executive Assistants (F2024N)
location: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description: Birmingham, West Midlands (England), B1 2AX : Bristol, South West England, BS1 6EH : Longstanton, East of England, CB24 3EN : Liverpool, North West England, L3 1BP : Leeds, Yorkshire and the Humber, LS11 9AT : Salford, North West England, M3 5AX : Newcastle upon Tyne, North East England, NE4 5BZJob SummaryReporting to the Professional Lead - Executive Assistants, the People Lead EA will manage the people aspects of up to 13 Executive Assistants.The Executive Assistants Team is responsible for enabling the effectiveness of Executive and Senior leaders by providing a professional, proactive Executive Assistant service which drives the need to work with impact and excellence every day.Job DescriptionThe position acts as the lead on all people-related aspects of the Team, including 1:1s, professional development, pastoral support, performance management and developing initiatives for continuous improvement. The People Lead is responsible for ensuring that the team have dedicated support, resulting in consistent, high level service delivery and both personal and professional development.Key relationships Has frequent contact with the Executive Leaders, Senior Leadership Team, Human Resources and Business Managers. Regular contact to develop, build and maintain effective relationships and networks with a range of stakeholders across the agencyKey Accountabilities And Responsibilities Develop, execute and maintain professional development plans aligning with the EA delivery framework, that optimises service delivery and which supports and provides impact to leaders. Implement and monitor capability plans, on a team and individual basis. Working with the EA community to progress continuous improvement initiatives. Regular communication and liaison with the Team, collectively and on a 1:1 basis. Management of Team meetings, 1:1’s, objective setting, career conversations and performance management. Management of team and individual performance, sharing success within the team and the business People management of the team to professionally develop the service and people in a collaborative and inclusive way. Build strong relationships with Senior Leaders through regular communication and active participation to ensure the service is optimised and needs are met. Lead, develop and maintain operational excellence while pursuing and embedding continuous improvement. Clear approach to incentivise behaviours that support transformation of the EA service, identifying and implementing targeted interventions to change culture, behaviours and mindsets at all levels. Ensure that appropriate cover is in place for periods of planned and unplanned EA absence to support the delivery of an uninterrupted service to Leaders. When required, provide surge capacity cover for EA activities, to ensure service demands are met.The position acts as the lead on all people-related aspects of the Team, including 1:1s, professional development, pastoral support, performance management and developing initiatives for continuous improvement. The People Lead is responsible for ensuring that the team have dedicated support, resulting in consistent, high level service delivery and both personal and professional development.Key relationships Has frequent contact with the Executive Leaders, Senior Leadership Team, Human Resources and Business Managers. Implement and monitor capability plans, on a team and individual basis. Working with the EA community to progress continuous improvement initiatives. Regular communication and liaison with the Team, collectively and on a 1:1 basis. Management of Team meetings, 1:1’s, objective setting, career conversations and performance management. Ensure that appropriate cover is in place for periods of planned and unplanned EA absence to support the delivery of an uninterrupted service to Leaders. When required, provide surge capacity cover for EA activities, to ensure service demands are met.Person specificationWe Are Looking For Someone Who Has: Experience as an executive level EA, with some experience as a team supervisor or lead The ability to think critically and strategically within the remit of the role as well as to solve problems while considering the impact of outcomes on the bigger picture Knowledge and experience of people management standards and the ability to provide professional input with credibility, generating confidence and enhancing delivery. Knowledge of successful ways of working to implement a consistent EA service to Leaders. Capability to coach and manage people and to have difficult and challenging conversations to ensure professional standards are being met. Working with colleagues to remediate issues and ensure appropriate decisions are made. Experience of developing systems and processes to support EA service delivery. Proven ability to work collaboratively in partnership and across organisational boundaries. The ability to create and set clear direction, motivating others to work towards a common goal. Ability to lead, manage and inspire a team in a collaborative and inclusive way. Capability to manage and develop professional staff, including the establishment of a positive performance culture and continuous service improvement recommendations.Homes England Is An Executive Non-departmental Public Body With Its Own Terms And Conditions, Benefits And Pension Scheme. We Are a Government Agency Working In Partnership With The Private Sector And Want To Employ And Retain Great People. title: Oliver Wyman - Executive Assistant - Newcastle
location: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description: The Opportunity: Reporting to the London EA Manager, the EA will provide executive level administrative support to up to 3 Partners.The position is a hybrid role with a minimum of 60% based in our Newcastle office with the other 40% working from home. Hours are 9:00am-6:00pm (including one hour lunch break).Key Responsibilities:Daily administration (60%)Maintain diaries for Partners, scheduling internal and external meetings, conference calls, video conferences, communicating with clients and infrastructure staff as appropriate.Coordinate travel and accommodation, book cars, rail tickets, etc.Prepare letters, proposals and other documents.Prepare monthly timesheets and expense reports for each assigned Partner.Assist with greeting clients, booking and setting up meeting rooms and organizing catering.Assist in organization of Partner files and offices.Assist the Marketing Department with the coordination of customized mailings.Client development (30%)Understand Partner’s key client and sales goals and support them by:Maintain list of contacts and business activities in address book and CRM database.Track and maintain sales activity/pipeline (e.g. who we have called upon, how far the discussions have progressed, what we have promised by when, and follow-up with Partners as necessary).Proactively spot clients that haven't been called upon and/or who need follow-up; arrange meetings accordingly.Coordinate travel efficiently by grouping client meetings where possible ("you're in Paris to see XYZ Company, shall I try to set up a meeting with X Company because we haven't spoken to them for three months").Develop excellent rapport with client assistants to further develop the client relationship.Execute requested follow-up calls for client mailings.Become familiar with the Practice themes and Oliver Wyman ‘products’ to develop confidence in setting up meetings with clients.Team Support (10%)Provide coverage for EAs when out of the office to ensure seamless support to PartnersProvide short-term coverage for Partners who may be in the midst of an EA assignment transition or who are new to the Firm.Provide training and support to new EAs.Participate in team meetings and projects with an eye to improving processes and the overall performance of the EA team.Experience required:Minimum of three years’ experience working in an administrative or customer service position.Experience in management consultancy, financial services, and/or a similar professional services industry a plus.Skills and Attributes:Strong service focus – dedicated to meeting the expectations of all staff through securing effective relationships with clients and executing deliverables to the highest standardsExcellent verbal and written communication skills and able to deal with people at all levels across a multicultural environment.Poise and judgmentAbility to maintain and respect confidentialityProactive team player who has experience of and has enjoyed working as part of a team. Willing to offer assistance when there is down time.Ability to juggle several tasks at once, to prioritize and manage own time - Not a clock watcher or someone who is unwilling to step outside their job descriptionSelf-starter, quick study, confidence and ability to work with little guidanceAbility to take constructive feedback in stride and incorporate feedback quickly.Ability to manage a heavy work volume and meet deadlines in an extremely fast paced environment.Methodical, organized, excellent attention to detail and stickler for accuracy.Flexible attitude, hard-working and results driven.Finally, we are looking for someone who will be committed to the company and add to the life and culture of Oliver Wyman.Technical Skills:Good Word, PowerPoint and Excel skills.Working knowledge of Outlook, a plus.Knowledge of CRM systems, a plus.Oliver Wyman is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information, visit oliverwyman.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age background, civil partnership status, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, gender reassignment, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable adjustments in accordance with applicable law to any candidate with a disability to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. If you have a disability that may require reasonable adjustments, please contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com.Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.R_335560

title: Executive Assistant And Business Administrator
location: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description: EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT AND BUSINESS ADMINISTRATOR - NEWCASTLE ON TYNE - FULLY ONSITE - UP TO £50k Robert Half are thrilled to be recruiting for a full-time, permanent Executive Assistant and Business Administrator for a client in Newcastle on Tyne. This role sits within the Business Administration team, who play a vital role in delivering service excellence across the organisation, providing essential support to multiple departments, the team manages meeting schedules, coordinates travel, oversees accounts, and delivers a variety of ad hoc projects. Their focus is on creating a supportive and efficient environment that enables colleagues to achieve their goals and drive the success of the business.Things to be know: Job Title: Executive Assistant and Business Administrator Salary: £35k - £50k, depending on experienceLocation: Newcastle on Tyne Working pattern: Full-time hours - Monday - Friday, 8am - 6pm, 5 days onsite Free onsite parkingResponsibilities: Manage diaries and stay on top of stakeholders' meeting schedules.Schedule meetings, handle logistics, and assist with meeting preparation.Prepare and format presentation materials.Screen and manage calls, coordinate conference calls, and handle correspondence with external contacts.Build strong working relationships across all departments, maintaining a professional and approachable manner at all times.Arrange business travel, including booking transport, accommodation, and preparing detailed itineraries.Support the planning, logistics, and delivery of firmwide events.Undertake ad hoc projects, research, administration, and record-keeping.Collaborate closely with the wider Business Administration and Executive Assistant teams to provide seamless support across the firm.Requirements: You'll have a minimum 2:1 Bachelor's degree (or international equivalent), supported by strong A-level results.Bring at least 2 years' experience in a similar administrative or support role.Highly proficient in Microsoft PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook, and Word, with excellent overall IT skills.Experienced in handling confidential information with professionalism, tact, and discretion.An exceptional communicator, both verbally and in writing.Proactive and solutions-focused, with the ability to keep projects moving and meet deadlines.Meticulous attention to detail and strong organisational skills.Comfortable managing multiple priorities and anticipating needs in a fast-paced environment.A strong team player with a positive attitude, energy, and a solid work ethic.Collaborative by nature, working effectively with colleagues across the wider Business Administration team.

title: [office Positions] Register Of Interest_SEUK-CL
description: DescriptionPosition at SUMITOMO ELECTRIC U.K. POWER CABLES LTD.Thank you very much for your interest. Here you can share your profile and CV forOffice positions. Future Vacancies Will Include But Not Exhausted ToHR Advisor, Finance Manager, Finance staff, Purchasing,IT, Admin etc..About Our Project In Port Of Nigg, HighlandSumitomo Electric is one of the leading global manufacturers of state-of-the-art high-end electricity transmission cables, up to and including 525kV HVDC(High Voltage Direct Current) cable.The new factory at Nigg, with investment value announced by the Scottish Government of £350M, will make a significant contribution towards realisation of the UK Net Zero Target by contributing to critical electricity transmission infrastructure to connect and deliver renewable energy, including offshore wind, to UK consumers and beyond. In addition, the factory will create more than 150 highly skilled jobs in the Scottish Highlands, with maximum utilisation of the local supply chain for production of cables and construction of transmission cable systems.In a significant boost for the UK green energy supply chain, Sumitomo Electric has commenced construction of its state-of-the-art subsea transmission cable factory in the Scottish Highlands. The factory will supply critical elements for the reinforcement of the UK electricity transmission grid and for connecting renewable energy production facilities to the grid.About Sumitomo Electric GroupSumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd. is a recognized global technology leader with over 290,000 employees working at around 400 group companies in 40 countries. Building on more than 120 years of innovation, today Sumitomo Electric is engaged in diversified business activities that support people’s daily lives and industries in five business segments: automotive, infocommunications, electronics, environment & energy, and industrial materials. To learn more about Sumitomo Electric, please visit https://sumitomoelectric.com and our YouTube Channel ( https://www.youtube.com/user/SumitomoElectric/ ). Follow us on LinkedIn ( https://www.linkedin.com/company/sumitomo-electric ).

title: Section Administrator
description: LocationInverness, GBDepartment NameScotlandAbout Network RailJoin Network Rail - Where People and Connections MatterAt Network Rail, we're dedicated to keeping passengers and freight moving safely and efficiently across the country. When you join us, you're not just part of a team - you're part of something that matters to millions. We believe that our people are at the heart of what we do, and every role plays a vital part in building a better, more connected railway. Click here to learn more.We're committed to creating a diverse, inclusive workplace that reflects the communities we serve. To discover more about our ED&I commitments click here. We value flexibility and understand the importance of a healthy work-life balance. As a Disability Confident Leader, we'll do everything we can to accommodate any needs throughout the recruitment process.If you're ready to make a real difference, we'd love to hear from you!Scotland's Railway is part of this large family and it covers a large geographical area from the Borders to Thurso at the far tip of the North East of Scotland, accounting for almost 20 per cent of the UK rail network. We provide rapid access along busy commuter routes to our seven cities, servicing the varied needs of our business and leisure commuter services, including cross border services and rural services. We have up to 50 freight services operating every day and we are steadily building capacity for this to grow. Our ambition to be responsive to passengers and freight users, drives us every day and we're empowered to do the right thing for those who use the rail network. We actively challenge unsafe practices and take responsibility for addressing risks, resolving issues and protecting safety and wellbeing.Brief DescriptionThe Section Administrator provides an accurate, efficient and effective administrative support to the line manager. They work as directed or using own initiative as the situation demands.We are seeking applicants with experience of using Microsoft Word, Excel and Power Point.About The Role (External) Key Accountabilities  Administration of rosters and timesheets. Accurately update systems, databases and records as required including opening/closing works orders where directed. Support the administration of the Assessment in The Line process. Set up and maintain efficient filing systems for the Section(s) paying particular attention to confidentiality where necessary and also those documents deemed to be safety critical. Undertake general office administration duties for the Section Manager using a range of knowledge and skills to provide effective support. Provide support and assistance to other Section Administrator positions as directed.Essential Job Skills, Experience and Qualifications  Experience of clerical support and office management Excellent time manager Knowledge of relevant IT systems Ability to work on own initiative Excellent interpersonal, communication and organisation skillsDesirable Knowledge of operational railway environmentHow To Apply (External)Click ‘apply’ to login and submit your CV.We're dedicated to creating an inclusive environment and we welcome applications from everyone!Network Rail is a Disability Confident Leader and we’ll try our best to adapt the process and offer a reasonable alternative to help support people with disabilities access, apply and interview for roles.You can visit Evenbreak’s Career Hive for advice on accessibility support if you’re unsure of the options available. Should you require any reasonable adjustments/modifications, please contact ScotlandResourcing@networkrail.co.uk .All offers of employment are conditional upon satisfactory completion of pre-employment checks. Click here for more informationKeeping people safe on the railway is at the heart of everything we do, safe behaviour is therefore a requirement of working for Network Rail. Applicants should demonstrate their personal commitment to safety in their application. Network Rail Benefits  – To find out about what benefits we offer, click hereNetwork Rail adheres to a structured pay framework, any salary offered will be within the pay range advertised. Grade:  Band 6 Salary:  £25,409 Closing date:  Midnight on Fri day 6th March 2026 - late applications will not be accepted. We retain the right to close the advert before the listed closing date (based on application volumes) so if you are interested in this vacancy, we encourage you to make your application as early as possible.

title: Receptionist
description: Bespoke Benefits… What’s in it for you?At Bespoke Hotels, our people are our biggest asset. We focus on engagement and individual development, working with natural strengths and supporting education from a commercial perspective. We create pathways so our people know the direction their careers will head.We believe that great hospitality can only be delivered by a happy, motivated and engaged team; so this is at the heart of our culture and of our ‘Be-Attitude’ values.We are proud to provide equal opportunities for our team members, encouraging inclusive and creative culture, providing long-term careers, supporting community and assisting in environmental matters.At Bespoke, we ensure meaningful relationships with our colleagues, guests and third-party suppliers, positively celebrating diversity and inclusion within our teams, encouraging collaboration and creativity.The RoleWe are currently looking for exceptional people to join our team and have a great opportunity for a ReceptionistAs a Receptionist you will be responsible for maximising room revenues in line with the Company’s standards, providing efficient and courteous service, to ensure total guest satisfaction.The ideal candidate would posses outstanding communication and organisation skills and have the ability to prioritise tasks. At least one years’ experience in a similar position is essential.Key Responsibilities Of The RoleTo be familiar with the days business, including arrivals, departures, and special requests.Deal with guests promptly upon arrival and departure, as per Company Standards for check in and out procedures.To have a good understanding of the guests’ requirements when they are checking in or out.To ensure all cash, charge, float and till procedures are carried out in accordance with company Policy.Demonstrates a working knowledge of all services and facilities of the hotel, as well as local area. Therefore, ensuring our guests are assisted effectively.Keeping the team up to date by attending the daily meetings.Maximise sales through up-selling, using incentives and promotionsEnsure all customer enquiries and requests for bookings are dealt with promptly, courteously and efficientlyEnsure prompt resolution of customer complaintsProfessionally and competently operates the telephone system in line with both operating and Company Standards.Requirements Of The RoleTo have worked in a hotel or with people in a customer related role.Excellent Customer service skills, to be able to follow standards and to have good organisational skills.To be able to make decisions in a fast paced environment and to enjoy itTo be able to communicate well to each other and guestsBe able to use a phone and computer system.Being BespokeBespoke Hotels was founded in 2000 and has consistently grown to the point where we now manage over 9,500 hotel rooms, 8,000 employees and £815 million of assets.We stand today as the UK’s Largest Independent Hotel Group, owning a number of hotels and partnering with an ever-increasing portfolio alongside this. Every agreement we have is Bespoke by name, and bespoke by nature. Our properties range from specialist golfing hotels, to award-winning spa resorts and chic city centre properties.We pride ourselves on a diverse roster and a unique, individualised management style, with over 90 management agreements across the UK, alongside a further 30 management consultancy services, and 100 hotels with sales & marketing services only.

title: Project Administrator
description: Base Location: For this role, we require successful applicants to be based Inverness with travel to projects in the Highlands and Islands and you'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in the office.Salary: £29,504 - £34,757 + a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family.Working Pattern: Permanent | Full Time | Flexible First options availableThe roleDue to ever-increasing workload, the Offshore Delivery Directorate within SSEN Transmission has an opportunity for a Project Administrator to join our expanding team and play a pivotal role in our project teams.The role will involve travelling to different sites/offices with the occasional night away. A driving license is essential.You will Register visitors to Office on system. Greet visitors and new starts on site. Assist the project teams with general administration duties, such as booking meeting rooms, PPE ordering, ordering stationery, dealing with office deliveries. Manage project trackers for site personnel and fleet vehicles. Support the Project Coordination Team to meet deadlines. Travel to projects as required.You have Excellent administration and organisational skills; this role is a busy one and will allow you an opportunity to learn about our business and interact with a wide range of stakeholders and project teams. You'll be competent using Microsoft Office packages and a flair for picking up new IT systems would be a bonus. Good literacy and communication skills, as well as excellent time management and organisation skills. An analytical and systematic approach to problem solving, be able to use own initiative, while also contributing to effective team working. A high regard for safety, of both yourself and your surroundings at all times is imperative.About SSESSE’s purpose is to provide energy needed today while building a better world of energy for tomorrow. We do this by developing, building, operating and investing in electricity infrastructure and businesses needed in the energy transition. Our Transforming for Growth investment plan sees us investing £33bn in critical electricity infrastructure across the five years to 2030.SSEN Transmission own and operate the electricity transmission network across the north of Scotland. We transport energy from where it is generated to where it is needed, ensuring a safe and reliable electricity supply for the communities we serve. But that's not all – we're upgrading the grid to deliver cleaner, homegrown energy for the future and building a network for net zero to create secure power for generations to come.Flexible Benefits To Fit Your LifeEnjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave.Work with an equal opportunity employerSSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact Hannah on hannah.e.cutajar@sse.com / 01738 341147 to discuss how we can support you.We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry.Ready to apply?Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. If you're offered a role with SSE, you'll need to complete a criminality check and a credit check before you start work.

title: Dispatcher
description: OverviewPeople. Passion. Pride. The company is committed to providing a safe working environment for all staff members. In all areas of our business there is a potential risk to the health, safety and welfare to everyone on our sites through the misuse of alcohol and drugs. As such the Company prohibits such misuse and carries out regular testing to enforce our Substance Misuse Policy.Please see the attached job description for further details on safety, security, wellbeing & compliance.What We Are Looking ForGood verbal communication.  A full 5 year work/unemployment/education history. Valid Passport Full UK Driving Licence Criminal Record Check and Airside Security Clearance DiversityMenzies Aviation are a committed equal opportunity employer and encourage applications for suitably qualified and eligible applicants regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marital status, pregnancy, and maternity. We strive to create an inclusive working environment, where the different knowledge, perspectives, experiences, and approaches of our global workforce are represented. Where everyone feels valued and can reach their full potential.Please be aware that as part of our recruitment process, we may look to use a variety of resourcing tools to help us understand your skills and experience in relation to the role.As part of our recruitment process, we will always consider how candidates fit with our values which you can learn more about here.Application InstructionsIs this role ticking all the boxes for you? If so, please click apply now!

title: Booking Agent
description: Bookings PartnerNewcastle-under-Lyme with hybrid working opportunities once fully trained£24,570 per annum pro ratePermanent, 15 hours a week workShifts run between 6am and 10pm with weekend work only and flexible working options during the week.Unlock your potential:Join our dynamic team at Bank Partners, where we are dedicated to delivering exceptional healthcare bookings services. We pride ourselves on putting customer service at the heart of everything we do.As a Bookings Partner, you will play a crucial role in ensuring our staffing services run smoothly. We are looking for a customer driven individual to drive our customer service efforts. By putting our customers at the heart of everything we do, you will be accountable for building candidate relationships, ensuring candidate retention, and driving fill rate by booking candidates into shifts.Bank Partners (part of Acacium Group) is an established, reputable, multi-award-winning business that provide high-quality, independent staffing and services to some of the largest trusts in the NHS. We work in partnership with each of our trusts to support staff shortages across the wards seamlessly and efficiently. Our highly skilled healthcare professionals work with us because we’re able to offer them opportunities at some of the most prestigious NHS Foundation Trusts and hospitals in the UK.Every day you will...Engage with candidates by proactively reaching out to check their availability, identify suitable candidates for priority shifts and encourage them to update their future availability.Keep an up to date understanding of candidates’ skills and experience as well as the expectations of management within the trusts.Collaborate with your Team Leader and Bank Business partners to gain insights and updates on priority areas, driving activity as needed.Address and resolve candidate queries promptly and efficientlySuccessfully drive booking activity, working closely with the Team Leader to achieve targetsWhat's in it for you?We love exceptional people, and in return, we offer an exciting place to work, with a wide range of benefits, rewards and opportunities to grow your career, including:Dedication to developing you and your career- we can give you the tools, guidance and support you need to be successful in your careerProgression and development opportunities across a global organisation with over 20 different brandsWorkplace pension schemeGym Flex- discounted gym memberships, UK-wide!Regular events and incentivesDiscounted Tastecard membershipReward Portal, offering discounts on shops, restaurants and barsFree flu vaccinationsCycle to Work schemeHealth Assessments and Dental InsuranceLife Assurance, Critical Illness Cover and Private Medical InsuranceJoin us and make a difference!If you want to join a global, marketing-leading organisation who are committed to the human side of business, then look no further. Acacium Group is a global healthcare solutions partner offering staffing, managed services and innovative delivery models to health and social care systems and the life sciences industry. We are a business with scale, leading digital capabilities and a strong financial profile, with a vision to be the leading global healthcare solutions partner.We are powered by the best people and have an unrivalled and diverse range of capabilities, all while incorporating our company values into everything we do - Putting People First, Always by your Side, Driven by Excellence.Join us and play a key part of shaping the future of society and improving people’s lives!To thrive in this role, you must ideally have...Previous experience in customer service or call handlingExcellent written and verbal communication skillsA high attention to detail and ability to thrive in a fast paced environmentProficient IT skills, including experience with MS Office packagesOur Inclusion Commitment:We are passionately committed to being a truly inclusive and diverse company- it is at the heart of our core value of Putting People First. We continue to enhance our culture of inclusion and diversity across all of our roles and actively encourage applications from all backgrounds and communities.#INDENT2022

title: Rota Co-ordinator
description: An exciting opportunity has arisen for a motivated individual to join the Medical Rota Team on a full time basis. We are looking for an enthusiastic person to join our friendly team to provide an efficient administrative and support service for Medical Workforce.The role involves being responsible for the management, day to day staffing, and coordination via health roster of the medical rotas, for various divisions. Assisting in the design preparation and publication of rotas.You must have good attention to detail together with the ability to prioritise your own work load to meet deadlines and show leadership and accountability for all issues pertaining to rotas.You will have good interpersonal skills liaising with Senior Management and Medical Staff.It is essential that the post holder be a competent user of Microsoft Office including Excel, you will be willing to adapt to change and learn new skills along with having a good knowledge of general administration and clerical procedures.The post holder should possess excellent organisational and communication skills whilst having the ability to deliver high quality support for our service and customers.The ability to remain calm under pressure, to work to tight deadlines and prioritise workload effectively is essential for this role.The post holder will support the divisions to ensure the provision of high-quality patient care by efficiently deploying appropriate staff.The post is instrumental in supporting the creation, implementation and management of staffing rotas across WTWA Services. We actively encourage personal development and access to training and as a result your own structured personal development plan will support your future development.Please note that the closing date is given as a guide, we reserve the right to close this vacancy once a sufficient number of applications have been received.Join Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust (MFT), the largest provider of specialist services, and our Oxford Road Campus in Manchester, the largest health academic campus in Europe – a great place to shape your career.With a workforce of over 30,000 colleagues, we work together to deliver exceptional care to more than 1 million people every year.Behind every patient cared for are people like you - keeping services running smoothly, supporting clinical teams, and helping create safe, welcoming environments for patients and our people alike.At MFT, we believe in the power of teamwork, respect, and inclusion. Our People Plan reflects our commitment to making MFT a place where everyone feels they belong, where your ideas are heard, and your contribution is truly valued. Join us and work smarter with our digital technology and thrive in a collaborative culture that empowers you to deliver your best to ensure that your work behind the scenes creates seamless, high-quality care and exceptional patient outcomes.Whether you're just starting out or looking to grow, we offer clear pathways for development and a culture that supports your wellbeing, ambition, and success. Everything you do makes a real difference to the lives of others.At MFT, your role matters, your impact is real, and you’re part of something bigger from day one.For further details / informal visits contact: Name: Katie Bergin Job title: Medical Workforce Manager Email address: Katie.bergin@mft.nhs.uk Telephone number: 01612915384

title: Volvo Receptionist / Host
description: About The RoleVolvo Cars Chester is looking for a friendly and enthusiastic Receptionist/Host to join our fantastic Welcome team!As a Sytner Host, you should share your passion for our brand every day and immerse customers in the world of Volvo.You will welcome every customer through our doors, managing their visit by directing them to the correct department efficiently and offering refreshments throughout. You will also manage incoming calls and will be responsible for connecting the diallers to the right department promptly. Our Receptionists/Hosts work a variety of full and part-time flexible patterns which can typically include weekends to ensure we provide our customers with the highest possible levels of service.About YouYou will be the first impression on the phone for our clients and guests – so a warm and cheerful voice with an ability to efficiently transfer the call is essential. You will also help proactively manage the experience of whoever visits our showroom so that each customer and guest feel that they have had uniquely personal and bespoke service.To provide the best service to our customers we'll need someone who is: a proactive communicator with our management and dealership teams, innovative in your approach to creating experiences and someone who will use their own initiative where they think it will delight a customer. You will also need to collate customer knowledge and anticipate their needs, to back this up, excellent organisational and preparation skills are required coupled with a genuine passion for detail.Why Sytner?Sytner Group are delighted to provide an industry-leading benefits package.We are passionate about continuous improvement and building an environment where everyone feels valued, appreciated and able to reach their full potential.Enhanced Holiday Entitlement – 33 days inc. bank holidaysIndustry-leading Maternity, Paternity and Adoption PayCareer DevelopmentRecognition of Long Service every 5 yearsDiscounted Car SchemesHigh Street DiscountsDiscounted Gym membershipsCycle to work schemeOne day a year paid voluntary / community workAt Sytner, our values and the way we behave are important to us. We are committed to creating an equitable working environment where we value and respect every individual's unique contribution, supporting our colleagues to thrive and achieve their full potential.As part of our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion, you have the right to ask for changes / adjustments to job interviews and the recruitment process. For more information around reasonable adjustments and the recruitment process please click here.Unsure? Read on…We represent over 23 of the world's most prestige vehicle brands, across our 140 UK dealerships.We pride ourselves on ‘Developing Talent and ‘Building Careers’ and our colleagues recently scored Sytner Group an outstanding 89% on our colleague engagement survey.Apply NowClose mapLocationVolvo ChesterSovereign Way, Sealand Rd, Chester, United Kingdom, CH1 4PHLoading...Open In Google Maps

title: Administration And Operations Officer
description: Stoke City Foundation, the official charitable arm of Stoke City Football Club, is seeking a dedicated and dynamic Administration and Operations Officer. This pivotal role will be based at the bet365 Stadium and other facilities as required.As a key member of the Operations team, reporting directly to the Operations Manager, you will play an essential role in driving the charity’s vision and mission. Your contributions will ensure that all initiatives align with the Foundation’s strategic objectives, enhancing our impact within the community.We're seeking a dynamic individual to be the pivotal first point of contact for visitors, callers, and partners of the Foundation. You will expertly manage all general enquiries via phone and email, acting as a key ambassador for our core values.Main ResponsibilitiesStreamlining processes to support delivery staff, Coordinators and the Senior Management Team.Managing and utilising the digital systems for efficient data, communication and operations.Producing regular operational reports on attendance, KPI’s and output for staff members.Overseeing all logistical aspects of Foundation events. Implementing efficient administrative processes for staff support.Reporting on operational and programme data.Preparing materials and support for all new staff members and volunteers. Promoting Club policies, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Code of Conduct.Designing and implementing governance and compliance processes in line with charity and funding legislation. Key Skills, Experience and QualificationsStrong organisational and administrative skills, including managing records, mail, supplies, bookings, and ensuring data accuracy.Experience in supporting operational processes, including scheduling, logistics, equipment management, and facility coordinationUnderstanding of safeguarding, health and safety, and risk assessment practices, with a willingness to undergo further training.Ability to coordinate events and programmes, including registrations, attendance tracking and venue preparation. Proficient in digital and office systems, including data entry, communication tools, and general IT applications.Experience in a charity, community, education, or sports environment in a senior administrative or operational capacity.Friendly, approachable, and customer-focused, with the ability to build positive relationships with colleagues, participants, and external partners.The ideal candidate will be available to work evenings and weekends to cover matchday fixtures and other adhoc events at the bet365 Stadium. The position will be offered on a full time and permanent basis.This organisation is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and adults at risk and expects all staff and volunteers to share this commitment. Background checks and DBS checks at the appropriate level will be obtained prior to employment commencing.Closing date for applications 17th March 2026.Stoke City Football Club endorses the principle of Equality and will strive to ensure that everyone who wishes to be involved in the Club will be treated fairly and with respect, regardless of their age, disability, gender reassignment, marital or civil partnership status, pregnancy or maternity, race, religion and belief, sex or sexual orientation. By applying to us you are agreeing to share your Personal Data in accordance with our Recruitment Privacy Policy which can be found at www.stokecityfc.com/recruitment-privacy-policy/The Club is committed to providing access and opportunities for all members of the community to take part in without threat of intimidation, victimisation, harassment, bullying and abuse. Any person associated with the Club can be assured of an environment in which their rights, dignity and individual worth are respected and in particular, that they are able to enjoy their engagement at the Club.

title: MAC COSMETICS - Melbourne International Airport - Full Time Beauty Advisor
description: DescriptionAs one of our highly skilled make-up artists you will combine your creative and technical expertise and passion for people to provide a welcoming, inspirational and personalized in-store experience which educates and delights our customers.You will also like working as part of a high performing team to create impact with in-store events and to ensure that the store always achieves our high standards of visual merchandising to stand out against our competitors.If you are a dynamic self-starter looking for a progressive career opportunity then this could be the perfect role for you and the first step of your career with a leader in prestige beauty.While certification in make up artistry and/or previous retail make up experience is desirable we also welcome applicants with amateur level experience. As a leader in prestige beauty with a culture that values diversity of thought and people, we offer excellent training and development and a competitive remuneration and benefits package.QualificationsWhile a qualification in make-up artistry/previous retail make up experience is preferred, we welcome applicants with amateur level experience who are able to demonstrate a high level of creative and technical expertise All applicants must be able to demonstrate the ability to provide inspirational, authentic and personalized customer serviceAbility to work retail hours including days, nights, weekends and special events in a fast-paced work environmentPrevious experience with retail point-of-sale software Applicants must be able to provide proof of right to live and work in the country if invited to attend for interview

description: Description🌟 Casual Stylist – TOM FORD BEAUTYLocation: Chadstone BoutiqueEmployment Type: CasualAre you passionate about luxury beauty, makeup artistry, and the art of fragrance? Refining beauty.

title: Full Time Beauty Advisor -emporium
description: The Body Shop Australia is currently recruiting for a Full Time Beauty Advisor who will be rostered to work 38 hours per week. On a Tuesday to Saturday Roster. Your role in a nutshellTo support the store management team in driving sales and profit within the store through the delivery of exceptional customer service on the shop floor at all times.Key DutiesThe Beauty Advisor will take the time to learn our customer’s needs and recommend fantastic products that make them feel so good. Uphold the values and vision of the company whilst ensuring adherence to company procedures & policies.Offer expert advice to our customers and provide an inspirational insight into The Body Shop products and philosophy.What We Look ForPrevious retail experience is preferredStrong interpersonal and communication skills with a particular focus on building rapport with customersA team player and be open to receiving daily coaching / feedback from your Store and Assistant Manager. A genuine passion for beauty and the retail industryConfident in reaching store targets and salesAn active contributor to our joyful collective by being an agile, tenacious and optimistic team member. Diversity StatementAt The Body Shop, we firmly believe that equality is a human right and that all people should have an equal opportunity to pursue their dreams and aspirations. We are committed to attracting, developing, and retaining the very best people by offering a creative and inclusive workplace where talent is recognized. We value diversity and consider all applicants for employment without regard to race, religion, colour, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status.
