title: Class 2 HGV Driver
description: Location: Halstead, EssexJob Type: Full Time, PermanentSalary: A GBP 33,000 : A GBP 37,000 DOE (including overtime and nights out)Hours: Day shifts, Monday to FridayIf youre friendly, hardworking, and energetic, this is a great opportunity to build a long:term driving career with a well:established, family:run business in Essex.Brooks Transport Services Ltd is a family:owned company with over 50 years experience operating in fast:moving commercial and consumer goods, including high:end, high:value products. Customer service is at the heart of everything we do, and we take pride in delivering a professional, reliable service without compromise.We are now looking for an experienced and positive Class 2 HGV Driver to join our team based in Halstead, Essex .The RoleAs a Class 2 HGV Driver, your responsibilities will include: Delivering and collecting goods as part of a UK distribution operation Loading vehicles to match the order of delivery drop:off points Planning and driving routes to ensure on:time deliveries Unloading goods and obtaining proof of delivery signatures Issuing invoices and accurately updating delivery, vehicle, and drivers hours records Recording mileage and fuel usageThis role involves 2:3 nights out per week .The position is based on a 48:hour working week , with overtime expected .About YouWe are looking for a professional driver who takes pride in their work and strives to deliver a first:class transport service. You must have: A current Class 2 (Category C) HGV licence A valid Digital Tachograph card A Driver CPC card Previous Class 2 driving experience (essential) A positive, efficient, and self:motivated attitude Strong communication, customer service, and interpersonal skills Excellent attention to detail and the ability to work independentlyWhat We Offer Competitive pay package A friendly, supportive team environment Long:term job security with an established, independent family business Ongoing support, training, and opportunities to progress

title: UGO Bus Driver
description: About The RoleUGO Bus DriverPermanent, Part TimeUp to £25,081 per annum (full time equivalent)Location: EssexWorking Style: Operational field-based workerJoin our UGO Bus Team!UGO Bus provides tailored transport for businesses, residents, and public sector organisations with our modern, accessible minibuses. As part of Essex County Council, one of the UK's largest local authorities, we pride ourselves on offering a friendly, dependable, and personable service.We’re looking for people with an outward looking, caring and professional approach. The ideal person will naturally go above and beyond; acknowledging that our passengers value a friendly face and someone who is thoughtful and considerate. All our drivers work on a split shift pattern, so flexibility is key. For example, a split shift can cover from 7.30am to 10am and 2.30pm to 5pm. Please note, the salary for part time and term time roles will be pro rata.We are currently looking for UGO Bus Driver across the following locations.Harlow - 25 hours per week Colchester - As and When Clacton 20 hour Term timeChelmsford 25 hoursClacton - As & WhenChelmsford - 28 hours Maldon - 28 hours Basildon - 20 hours Colchester - 20 hours As a Ugobus driver, you’ll be working with a diverse range of passengers; working with students through to older people, and individuals with disabilities or special educational needs.To find out more information about our UGO Bus service, please watch our video: UGO Bus - more than just a journeyWe Are Looking For Someone WhoHold a valid driving license with a D1 entitlement - EssentialExperience in working with customers from a broad and diverse range of backgrounds/needsCan demonstrate prior experience of being adaptable and flexible to business pressuresAbility to work under pressure and remain a calm and professional approachAbility to use a smartphone and/or tabletDemonstrate good communication and social skills in a customer-based settingIf successful, you will need to complete an enhanced DBS check, undertake an ECC minibus driver’s assessment and attend mandatory training; including, but not limited to MiDAS and First AidWhy Essex?As one of the largest and most dynamic UK local authorities, Essex offers a stimulating public service environment. Covering over 1,420 square miles and serving 1.8 million residents, we’re committed to transformational change and delivering better quality at lower cost. Recognised in the top three of the latest IMPOWER index for productivity, we ensure everyone matters in Everyone's Essex. With flexible working options and a focus on innovation, whether you are looking to develop your career or contribute to the local community, Essex is the ideal place for you.In February 2025, Greater Essex was accepted onto the Government’s devolution priority programme, which will see local authorities in Essex work towards establishing a Mayoral Combined County Authority by May 2026. You can read more about devolution and local government reorganisation (LGR) on our careers site.Essex County Council is proud to be a Disability Confident Leader. We’re committed to interview all disabled applicants who choose to apply under our Job Interview Scheme if they meet the minimum requirements of the job. Please speak to our Recruiters if you require any reasonable adjustments during the process. We’re also committed to interview all veterans and care leavers, who meet the minimum requirements of the role for which they are applying.Once you’ve secured your role within Essex County Council you will receive many great employee benefits, including support for unpaid carers and flexible working arrangements to accommodate their needs.At Essex County Council, we value integrity and ethical behaviour. We kindly ask that you embrace our code of conduct and the Nolan principles, including throughout the recruitment process. Your commitment to these standards helps us maintain a positive and respectful workplace for everyone.As part of employment with Essex County Council (ECC) should your role require a DBS check you will be required to register with the DBS update service. The DBS update service ensures all registered DBS certificates are kept up to date and allows authorised parties to view the status of your certificate. This is an annual subscription, and you will be responsible for ensuring this is maintained. For further information on the DBS update service please go to https://www.gov.uk/dbs-update-serviceEssex County Council is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults, and expects all employees and volunteers to share this commitment.If you would like to be kept up to date on other relevant opportunities, blogs, news, events and industry related news at Essex County Council, please join The Essex Talent CommunityIf you have queries on this role or require anything further, please email resourcing.team@essex.gov.ukFollow us on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn. Further details can be found on our careers page.DownloadsPay and Reward | Our Ways of Working  | About Us  | Organisation Behaviours

title: Brand Manager - Kenco, L'or, Douwe Egberts
description: Company DescriptionWho we are?JDE Peet’s is the world's leading pure-play coffee and tea company, with local roots dating back to 1753, and is headquartered in the Netherlands. We unleash the possibilities of coffee and tea in more than 100 countries with a portfolio of over 50 brands, including Kenco, L’OR, Peet’s, Jacobs, Senseo, Tassimo, Douwe Egberts, OldTown, Super, Pickwick and Moccona.What’s it like to work at JDE Peet’s?We are people united by the power and possibility of tea and coffee. At JDE Peet’s, we thrive on the freedom to pursue opportunities, provoking new ideas to make an impact at scale. Our ambition is simple – JDE Peet's: unleashing the possibilities of coffee and tea.We bring action to our intentions, following through on what was agreed, always pushing things forward. We offer our people a range of learning and development programmes to support their personal growth.At JDE Peet's, we believe it’s amazing what can happen over a cup of coffee or tea.Apply to unleash a cup of amazing for your career. UNLEASH YOUR POSSIBILITY! Find out more at http://careers.jdepeets.comJob DescriptionWe’re looking for a Brand Manager to join our UK Marketing team and lead growth for our iconic instant coffee brands - Kenco, L’OR, and Douwe Egberts. This role is perfect for someone passionate, analytical, creative, and entrepreneurial. You’ll shape the future of the category through breakthrough innovation, strategic thinking, and impactful execution, bringing consumer insights to life on the shelves of the UK’s biggest retailers.Key ResponsibilitiesSet the future vision for the portfolio Define long-term brand positioning and portfolio strategy, ensuring alignment with consumer trends and business objectives.Lead the future innovation strategy Identify whitespace opportunities, develop pipeline plans, and champion disruptive ideas that drive category growth.Accelerate recruitment of the next generation of coffee drinkers Create targeted initiatives and campaigns to attract younger consumers and new segments into the category.Deliver breakthrough renovation and innovation plans Manage projects from concept to launch, including product development, packaging, and go-to-market execution.Respond to consumer, category, and customer performance & trends Monitor market data translate insights into actionable strategies and adapt plans to changing dynamics.Drive brand communication and activation Collaborate with the equity and communication team to develop compelling campaigns across digital, in-store, and experiential channels.Collaboration and partnership across functions and with global teams Work closely with Sales, Category, Supply Chain, and Global Marketing to ensure seamless delivery and alignment.Manage financial performance and business cases Build robust business cases for innovation, track P&L impact, and ensure projects deliver against agreed KPIs.QualificationsAt JDE Peet’s, we move fast and think big. We’re a hands-on, high-energy team driven by entrepreneurial spirit. If you’re hungry to learn, keen to build brands from the inside out, and not afraid to take initiative, you’ll thrive in this role.What We're Looking ForPrior experience in Marketing, ideally within FMCG, with a strong understanding of brand building and portfolio management.Hands-on exposure to digital marketing and shopper activation, including development and execution of campaigns.Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and turn insights into actionable strategies.Proven project management capabilities, from planning through to delivery, managing multiple stakeholders.Excellent communication skills and a collaborative, relationship-first mindset to work effectively across functions and with global teams.A proactive, self-starter attitude -comfortable navigating ambiguity and driving initiatives forward.A “can-do” spirit with agility to adapt in a fast-paced, growth-driven environment.Additional InformationYour application at JDE Peet'sIf you are interested in this career opportunity at JDE Peet's, apply and join us. After receiving your application we will do our utmost to give you quick feedback. Part of the recruitment procedure at JDE Peet's would be an online assessment, followed by interviews. A pre-employment check can be part of the recruitment process.Our DE&I StatementAt JDE Peet’s we aim to create a better future where we authentically serve, reflect and embrace everyone. We believe in fostering a more diverse, equitable and inclusive organisation where everyone feels comfortable to truly be who they are and unleash their full potential. This means we do not tolerate discrimination on the basis of our race, ethnicity, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, age, disability, or any other trait protected by law.Throughout the entire employee lifecycle, we place DE&I at the core of our people processes. This enables us to drive objectivity and eliminate any potential bias, ensuring an inclusive experience in the workplace. Do not hesitate to reach out if you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process.The future of work is hybrid, with both office and home playing an important role. At JDE Peet's, we value meeting colleagues in the office to unleash the possibilities of coffee and tea together and we enjoy our focus time when working from home. Join us and you will experience both. Because it’s amazing what happens over a cup of coffee.#unleashyourpossibility #acupofamazing

title: Brand Manager
description: No Relocation Assistance OfferedJob Number #170550 - Surrey, England, United KingdomWho We AreColgate-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name!Join Colgate-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all.Location: Woking UKTeam: Professional Marketing, Customer Experience Reporting to: Group Brand Manager, Northern Europe Are you ready to drive market leadership and truly make an impact on oral health across Northern Europe?We are seeking an enthusiastic, strategic, and collaborative Professional Brand Manager to lead our key therapeutic and consumer toothpaste portfolio. This isn't just a marketing role—it's an opportunity to be a category expert and influencer, shaping professional recommendation and market growth for essential oral care products.You'll be instrumental in establishing our brand as the leader in everyday caries and sensitivity prevention, directly impacting millions of lives through dental professional advocacy. If you thrive in a dynamic, international environment and are passionate about building strong relationships, we want to hear from you!What You'll Be Leading: The Purpose of Your RoleAs the Professional Brand Manager, you will be the visionary and commercial driver behind our professionally-focused toothpaste portfolio across the Northern Europe Hub (UK, Ireland, Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Finland).Market Leadership: Drive all key performance indicators (KPIs), including market share growth, net sales, margin, and professional recommendation objectives. Professional Advocacy: Strategically grow awareness and understanding of our product benefits among the dental profession to inspire and drive chairside recommendation. Strategic Growth: Partner with the European team to assess opportunities, build a robust 5-year innovation pipeline, and expand market demand. External Influence: Liaise with and influence key external collaborators and Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) to build brand advocacy and inform our strategic direction. Go-to-Market Ownership: Lead the end-to-end commercialisation and go-to-market strategy for all new product launches across Northern Europe. Your Core Contributions: Key ResponsibilitiesYou will own the annual brand plan and execute high-impact initiatives that drive our commercial success:Brand Strategy: Own the development and execution of the annual brand plan, securing alignment and consensus across all internal partners. Commercial Execution: Create and implement targeted initiatives to drive volume and net sales growth across the Northern Europe Hub. Stakeholder Collaboration: Serve as the primary communication point for the portfolio, developing compelling training and detailing materials for our internal teams (e.g., Oral Care Consultants, Customer Development). Innovation & Launch: Lead the progression of NPD projects with the EU team and spearhead the local development and implementation of the go-to-market plan, including business case creation, forecasting, and gaining launch approval. Partnerships & Insights: Collaborate closely with the N. Europe Professional Group Brand Manager and external agencies to identify market expansion opportunities using deep market insights. Budget Management: Strategically manage the advertising and promotions (A&P) budget to ensure maximum return on investment. Training & Empowerment: Partner with the Scientific Affairs team to lead comprehensive product training for our Oral Care Consultants, ensuring they are fully equipped with product and clinical knowledge. Risk Management: Provide timely and transparent communication on all brand opportunities and challenges to the Group Brand Manager. What You Bring: Your Professional ProfileIf you have a passion for healthcare, a strong commercial mindset, and are excited by a challenge, we encourage you to apply—even if you don't check every box!Education: A bachelor's degree is highly valued. Experience: Previous experience in dental, healthcare, pharmaceutical marketing, or consumer health is a plus. Foundational Knowledge: A solid foundation in basic (oral) health or life sciences is essential for success in this professional environment. Commercial Acumen: Highly competent commercial and analytical skills with a track record of using data and insights to make informed decisions. Leadership & Influence: A confident and compelling presence that quickly builds trust and respect with diverse internal teams and external dental professionals. You have the gravitas to challenge the status quo and successfully influence stakeholders at all levels. Mindset: Inquisitive, proactive, and a true team player who naturally seeks out opportunities for improvement and enthusiastically motivates others. Key Competencies:Professional Partnership & Relationship Building: Exceptional networking skills to build mutually beneficial relationships. Strategic Vision: The ability to balance achieving both short-term targets and long-term strategic goals. Effective Communication: Clearly, concisely, and convincingly expressing ideas to various audiences. Leading Change: Demonstrating flexibility and confidence when initiating and navigating change. Our Commitment to InclusionOur journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business.Equal Opportunity EmployerColgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law.Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.site

title: Brand Manager - Tassimo
description: Company DescriptionFor more than 265 years, JACOBS DOUWE EGBERTS (JDE) is inspired by its belief that it’s amazing what can happen over a cup of coffee or tea. We are fuelled by our purpose of unleashing the possibilities of coffee and tea to create a better future. Today, our coffee & tea portfolio is available in more than 100 developed and emerging markets, through a portfolio of over 50 brands that collectively cover the entire category landscape led by leading household names such as L’OR, Jacobs, Senseo, Tassimo, Douwe Egberts, Old Town, Super, Pickwick and Moccona.JDE is part of JDE Peet’s, the world’s largest pure-play coffee and tea company, headquartered in The Netherlands.What’s it like to work at JDE?We are proud of our passionate, driven associates that challenge the status quo and pursue mastery in everything they do. Our goal is simple and ambitious – JDE: A coffee & tea for every cup.At JDE, we're Made to Stand Out. Every day we are progressive in outlook, ambitious in nature, resourceful in action and decisive in approach, bringing coffee and tea moments to everyone around the world.Job DescriptionThis rare BM role is the perfect mix of portfolio management, execution and activation. The Brand Manager will be responsible for managing the TASSIMO product portfolio, including brands Costa, Kenco, L’OR and Cadbury. As the number one brand in the Single Serve category, Tassimo plays a leading role in delivering the quality, variety and convenience that consumers are looking for. We are looking for a passionate, entrepreneurial & confident Brand Manager to join our team and make an impact at shaping the future!Key Responsibilities Of The RoleResponsible for portfolio management and cross brand initiatives. Links in with L’OR and Kenco brand teams to ensure category and brand strategy alignment.Supports the SBM & Marketing Lead with generation of NPD ideas with category, consumer and shopper insights at the heart. Ensures project progress into product development and implementation phase.Leads cross-functional teams to deliver local NPD projects, execution of brand/ shopper initiatives and demonstrates project management capabilities and executes with excellence.Responsible for development and execution of through the line activations, including ATL, PR & shopper.Ownership of ongoing brand & portfolio performance monitoring & reporting.Demonstrates category leadership, is a go to person when it comes to interaction with local and global stakeholders. Responsible for key stakeholder alignment on strategy, project alignment and approval meetingsWorks with the Global Category team, representing the UK interests and helping influence the global agenda to fit UK needsFull A&P budget managementQualificationsQualificationsA passionate brand advocate with a natural affinity for brands and how they connect with consumersBrand Management experience with evidence of leading major product launchesConfident delivering presentations both internally & externallyProven track record in innovation development including project managementAble to demonstrate how you can inspire & engage teams behind ideas to make things happen.Ability to influence senior stakeholdersArticulate communicator, verbally and in presentationsProactive and challenging, showing boundless curiosity.Analytical, inquisitive and innovativeEducation: to degree level preferred

title: Radiographer
description: For further information about this role, please contact Elizabeth Bartram (Team Leader) on 01924 541571About The RoleTo work as a member of the plain film radiography team responsible for producing high quality diagnostic images within the radiology service.For More Information Please See Attached Job Description.Main dutiesTo perform a comprehensive range of radiographic procedures, without supervision, both within the department and externally e.g. theatre, wards, etc.To contribute towards the efficient deployment of resources; enabling the patient to receive a safe accurate and timely examination.The post holder will be required to participate in the out of hours rota undertaken by the department and will assist in covering any non-rostered duties by mutual agreement between the post holder and the radiology manager.To communicate effectively with patients and carers of varying abilities and understanding; explaining procedures in order to gain co-operation.To re-assure and encourage patients and carers.To liaise with multidisciplinary teams regarding examination needs and results.To build and maintain good relationships within the team and other departments and disciplines, working together to develop service improvements.To ensure the safe use of x-ray and associated equipment.To report equipment malfunctions to senior staff and to help maintain comprehensive and accurate records of faults; ensuring that staff are kept up to date as to the working status of equipment.To develop your understanding of IR(ME)R and IRR99 regulations: working closely with the Radiation Protection Supervisors to continuously evaluate and monitor systems and practices.To input, store and provide patient information on the directorate Radiology Information System maintaining the integrity and quality of the data consistent with legislation, policies and procedures.Essential Person Specification RequirementsBSc Radiography or D.C.R.(R)HPC. REGISTEREDAbility to undertake all general radiographyPrevious NHS experienceKnowledge of radiation safety regulations and relevant protocolsAbility to work out of hours - 30 min on call response timeFlexible according to the demands of the servicePlease see attached job description for full list of essential and desirable criteria.Be part of MY teamWe are an acute trust caring for over one million people in Wakefield and Kirklees. Our 10,000 staff work in patients’ homes, the community, and our three hospitals in Pontefract, Dewsbury, and Pinderfields (Wakefield). We prioritise our people and values so we can deliver the best possible care to patients. Our team is friendly, passionate, and innovative, always seeking better ways to work.We support work-life balance and foster a diverse, inclusive environment where everyone belongs. Our staff networks offer safe spaces to share ideas and concerns so we can increase awareness and appreciation of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and especially encourage members of the LGBTQ+ community, ethnic minority groups, and people with disabilities or neurodivergence to apply, as they are currently underrepresented in the Trust.If you value caring, high standards, improvement, and respect, join the MY team to make a difference every day.Application GuidanceWe understand that more applicants are using AI technology to help with their applications. While these tools can be useful, they cannot fully capture your skills, knowledge, and experience. It’s important that you personalise your application. Relying solely on AI is not recommended, as it may reduce your chances of success. Our screening process is thorough, so if you have used AI, please make sure to disclose this on your application form.Our BenefitsAccess to the NHS pension planGenerous holiday allowance of 27 days plus bank holidays, increasing with 5 and 10 years of serviceExceptional employee health and wellbeing servicesExtensive benefits and support, including:Onsite nurseries and childcare salary sacrifice schemeCycle to work schemeHome electronics schemeCar lease salary sacrifice schemeWorking carers support and advice, carers network and carers passportFlexible working options and family and carer-friendly policiesEstablished staff networks, including LGBTQ+ and Race EqualityCareer progression, training and supportSkilled worker sponsorshipYou can find out more about skilled worker and health and care visas on our .

title: Radiographer
description: RadiographerNuffield Health Leeds Hospital | RadiologyPermanent | Full-Time (37.5 Hours) | On Call RequiredCompetitive Salary (DOE)State-of-the-art equipment and more time to focus on what matters. We’re the UK’s largest Healthcare Charity and this is the career you’ve always wanted. Become our new addition to the team and we’ll ask you to see each patient as an individual, carrying out your role with understanding and empathy.As a Radiographer at our Leeds Hospital, you’ll bring high levels of technical knowledge and the ability to work quickly and accurately. You have a great eye for detail and you’re caring by nature. You will hold a BSC degree in diagnostic radiography (or equivalent), and have excellent teamwork skills.As a Radiographer, you will:· Help deliver our high-quality diagnostic imaging service· Experience a varied caseload in the department, on the ward and in theatre· Work with leading practitioners and new practices, equipment and facilities· Carry out scoping to develop policies and procedures· Supervise and develop junior colleagues· Ensure that what you do is delivered with sensitivity and compassionIn the successful candidate we are looking for:Ability to work autonomously and coordinate/prioritise X-ray/Fluoroscopy workload to maximise patient safety and imaging efficiency.Enthusiastic, pragmatic and proactive professional; one that can manage daily, weekly and monthly tasks/checklists and successfully navigate peaks and troughs in workload to complete these effectively.Hardworking individual that thrives in both small modality based, and larger department wide team efforts to achieve high quality work and patient satisfaction.Keen interest in and/or a drive to further develop auditing skills as part of a robust yearly audit calendar across all modalities, focused on optimising patient dose and image quality. Helping you feel good.We want you to love coming to work, feeling healthy, happy and valued. That’s why we’ve developed a benefits package with you in mind. Choose from private healthcare, financial advice, a cycle to work scheme and more. At Nuffield Health, we take care of what’s important to you.Nuffield Health Leeds HospitalNuffield Health Leeds Hospital is proud to have received an "Outstanding" rating from the health care regulator, the Care Quality Commission (CQC), following an inspection in February 2017. The CQC highlighted that patient care was at the heart of the service and this is testimony to the commitment, skill, and pride of the team at the hospital.Our successful, purpose-built hospital deals with complex surgery and a vast range of specialities involving some 300 consultants. There are 80 bedrooms, an 8 bed Level 3 critical care unit and 6 theatres (2 Stryker laparoscopic and 2 laminar flow). We are close to the city centre and central railway station.Join Nuffield Health and create the future you want, today.If you like what you see, why not start your application now? We consider applications as we receive them and reserve the right to close adverts early (for example, where we have received an unprecedented high volume of applications). So, it’s a good idea to apply right away to ensure you’re considered for this role.It starts with you.

title: Administrator

with ordering materials, resources and plantWork alongside Site managers with key weekly paid and agency labour processes (e.g. timesheet collection, coding, collecting overtime requests)Use the chosen accounting system for processing project transactions and running reportsRun weekly management order reports, review and attend meetings with the Project Manager, Quantity Surveyor where numbersPromote a culture of Continuous Improvement and of sharing best practice amongst the teamStill interested , does this sound like you?Relevant experience in a transaction process role (AP, scheme etcAbout MurphyMurphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Gold Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. title: PA / HR Administrator

We are proud of our growth record, our position as cutting-edge in our industry and our ethos, values, and culture.Last year showed us up 20% on orders compared to the previous year, and we have an ambitious growth strategy, supported by recently launched products and an exciting pipeline of innovations. As preferred or framework suppliers to Scottish, Welsh, Northumbrian, Northern Ireland and Irish Water, we are firmly established as a trusted name in the industry.We are now looking for a PA / HR Administrator to join us for a 12 month fixed-term contract with the potential to become permanent. We are open to full-time (35 hours per week) and part-time (25 hours per week) candidates.The Benefits Salary of £27,000 - £32,000 per annum, DOE (pro rata for part-time) Annual company performance-related bonus Group Life Scheme (x4 salary) Company uniform Ongoing training and professional development Employee Assistance Programme Regular team bonding and social events Free broaden your skills, build commercial awareness and develop a strong understanding of how a successful manufacturing business operates from the inside out.Whats more, with the choice of full-time or part-time hours, you can shape the role around your lifestyle, while enjoying a competitive salary, performance-related bonus and ongoing professional development within a friendly environment that values teamwork and growth.So, if youre looking for flexibility, variety and real exposure to the workings of a leading business, read on and apply today!The Role As our PA / HR Administrator, you requests efficiently and professionally within a supportive, open-plan office environment.You will learn by working directly alongside the MD, gaining exposure to different areas of the business and We are proud of our products, growth record, company culture, and values of Fairness, Integrity, Respect and Customer Satisfaction.We are looking for a Graduate Web Application Developer to join us on a full-time, permanent basis.This is the ideal opportunity for a talented developer with both frontend and backend expertise to build their career with our outstanding company, where there is plenty of scope and support to grow and build a professional portfolio to be proud of.What We Offer Salary of £28,000 per year Bonus annual company performance -related bonus Ongoing training and professional development. Working hours 35 hours per week, 8.30am to 4.30pm Group life scheme (4 x Salary) Annual team bonding and social events Employee assistance programme Free parking Company uniformAbout The RoleAs a Graduate Web Application Developer, you will Develop and maintain our in-house cloud-based management system (PIMA) Develop and maintain our remote access via MQTT to our instruments (Control Insite) Support and develop existing online data management products. Help to develop a new data management cloud-based product to manage data Help support the internal IT systemsAbout YouTo be considered as a Graduate Web Application Developer, youll need experience of using the following tools: Server Backend: TypeScript (JavaScript), PostgreSQL, TypeORM, PostGraphile and/or Rust Web Frontend: Angular (Ionic, SPA, React, and/or TypeScript), Bootstrap (CSS, SCSS and/or SASS) and HTMLIdeally, We Are Looking For Someone Who Can Can work independently and as part of a team Have strong problem-solving and innovative thinking skills Take pride in your work and have good attention to detail Have reasonable interpersonal skills and the ability to disseminate knowledge Have the ability to work to tight deadlinesOther organisations may call this role Graduate Developer, Junior Application Developer, Graduate Web Developer, Junior Web Developer, or Graduate Application Developer.What comes next?We believe interviews should work both ways, so we invite shortlisted candidates to come and visit our site, get to grips with our products and meet our lovely team.This gives you a chance to see if the company is a good fit for you, as well as if you are a fit for us, all before the possibility of job offers.Webrecruit and Process Instruments are equal opportunities employers, value diversity and are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all employees and all applicants for employment. title: Frontend Developer (12-month Fixed-term Contract)
description: Who We AreScience In Sport Group is a world leader in sports and performance nutrition.We have two highly regarded brands; SiS which combines world-class science and innovation to provide optimal performance nutritional solutions for professional athletes, sports and fitness enthusiasts and the active lifestyle community. This includes over 320 professional teams worldwide across a broad range of sports including Football, Rugby, Cycling, Athletics, Basketball, Netball and Swimming. Brand ambassadors include former track cyclist Sir Chris Hoy, an eleven-time world champion and six-time Olympic champion, and Eilish McColgan, who won gold in the 2022 Commonwealth Games 10,000 metres.PhD Nutrition is a premium, active-nutrition brand for the active lifestyle community wanting intelligent nutrition to help them achieve their goals. With over 20 years of research and development, our products are designed to maximise performance in and out of the gym. It’s an exciting time for PhD Nutrition as 2025 will kick off an entirely new line up of brand ambassadors, from hybrid athletes to qualified sports nutritionists.The two brands sell through the phd.com and scienceinsport.com digital platforms, third-party online marketplaces including Amazon, as well as extensive domestic and international retail distribution.In 2022 the company opened a state of the art 160,000 square foot manufacturing and logistics facility in Blackburn, Lancashire. Providing a significant advantage in product costs and customer response time and most importantly, allowing Science In Sport to lead the way for quality standards.Science In Sport is the nutrition partner to globally recognised elite teams, including Tottenham Hotspur, Saracens Rugby, British Triathlon, Aquatics GB, British Cycling, England Netball and Loughborough Sports. As well as being the Official Energy Gel Provider of RunThrough and the Official Nutrition Partner of Ultra X.The RoleContract: 12-month Fixed-term contractSalary: £40,000 - £50,000 per annum)Working ResponsibilitiesBuild and maintain Adobe Commerce storefronts - Develop responsive, high-quality themes, templates, and UI components using Adobe Commerce’s frontend framework.Translate designs into functional interfaces - Implement UX/UI designs, wireframes, and prototypes into clean, efficient frontend code.Optimise site performance and user experience - Deliver fast page loads, strong Core Web Vitals, accessibility compliance, and a consistent cross-device experience.Troubleshoot, enhance, and support the storefront - Resolve frontend issues, improve existing components, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to support smooth releases.What You Are Responsible For DeliveringA polished, performant Adobe Commerce storefront that works across browsers and devices.Clean, scalable frontend code that aligns with Adobe Commerce best practices.Reliable delivery of features, enhancements, and fixes within sprint timelines.Proper integration and styling of Adobe Commerce extensions and custom modules.Reduced frontend defects through proactive testing, performance tuning, and peer collaboration.Transparent communication around progress, blockers, and technical decisions.The skills and behaviours required to do this job wellTechnical SkillsStrong command of HTML5, CSS3, LESS/SASS, JavaScript, and jQuery.Thorough understanding of Adobe Commerce (Magento 2) theme structure, layout XML, PHTML, and Knockout.js.Hands-on experience with UI Components and Adobe Commerce’s rendering architecture.Proficiency with Git, build tools (Webpack/Gulp/Grunt), and browser debugging.Ability to improve frontend performance, SEO, and accessibility.BehavioursWrites organised, maintainable code and follow established workflows.Communicates clearly with designers, backend developers, and project managers.Takes ownership — follows tasks through from definition to completion.Prioritises effectively in fast-paced, Agile environments.Identifies issues early and proposes practical, technical solutions.Curious, inquisitive and actively researches industry trends, updates to Adobe Commerce, release notes.Performance – how you will know you are doing a great jobThe storefront delivers fast, stable performance with strong Core Web Vitals and Lighthouse scores.Designs are accurately implemented and aligned with branding and UX expectations.Frontend-related bugs are minimal and decreasing over time.Sprint commitments are met consistently with high-quality output.Teammates and stakeholders rely on your expertise and trust your work.Customer experience and site usability are noticeably improved.Specific metrics will be set when in role and will be reviewed and built upon regularly. Alongside this, you will be supported with a personal performance plan that will enable you to continue improving your own performance and impact.Experience and mindset that will enable you to do well in this role.2–5+ years of experience working with Adobe Commerce (Magento 2) frontend development.Strong understanding of eCommerce UX principles and customer behaviour.Detail-oriented mindset with a strong focus on performance and quality.Eagerness to learn, iterate, and stay current with Adobe Commerce updates.Collaborative, solution-focused attitude with openness to feedback.Practical problem solver — able to break down issues and deliver clear solutions.Experience working with an agency (desirable)Science in Sport Group is an equal opportunity employer; we value diversity and strive to cultivate an inclusive workplace. We do not tolerate discrimination of any kind in regard to race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
